function: dameraulevenshtein(s1, s2, int insdel, int rep, int trans)
sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT PRIMARY KEY, str1 VARCHAR(255), str2 VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, str1, str2) VALUES (1, 'MonetDB', 'DB'), (2, 'Test', 'Tests');
SELECT id, dameraulevenshtein(str1, str2, 3, 1, 2) / 2.0 AS distance FROM test_strings WHERE distance IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, (dameraulevenshtein(str1, str2, 5, 2, 1) / NULLIF(LENGTH(str1), 0)) AS normalized_distance FROM test_strings HAVING normalized_distance > 0.5;
CREATE TABLE numbers (num1 DECIMAL(10, 2), num2 DECIMAL(10, 2));
INSERT INTO numbers (num1, num2) VALUES (NULL, 1.0), (3.0, NULL), (0.0, 2.0), (5.5, 2.5);
SELECT num1, num2, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE dameraulevenshtein(str1, str2, 1, 1, 1) > 2) / (num1 + num2) AS ratio FROM numbers WHERE num1 IS NOT NULL OR num2 IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE complex_data (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, group_id INT, value VARCHAR(255));
SELECT group_id, SUM(CASE WHEN dameraulevenshtein(value, 'xyz', 1, 2, 1) < 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS close_matches FROM complex_data GROUP BY group_id HAVING close_matches > 1;
SELECT id, dameraulevenshtein(UPPER(value), 'AZ', 1, 1, 2) / 2.5 AS distance_ratio FROM complex_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL ORDER BY distance_ratio DESC;
SELECT num1, num2, dameraulevenshtein(CONCAT(CAST(num1 AS VARCHAR), 'text'), 'num2', 1, 1, 1) AS edit_distance FROM numbers WHERE num1 IS NOT NULL AND num2 IS NOT NULL;
